---
title: "Students"
pagetitle: "Students | Peleg Kremer"
description: "Current and former graduate and undergraduate researchers."
---

::: {.lead}
Working with students is one of the most rewarding parts of this job. Below are current advisees and alumni, with brief project descriptions. If you are a prospective student interested in spatial analysis of urban systems, please be in touch.
:::

## Current graduate students

- **Charles Wildonger** – Heavy metal contamination and transport in green stormwater infrastructure.
- **Mac Sanders** – Observational analysis of human activity, physical conditions, and social context around GSI sites in Kensington, Philadelphia.
- **Prottoy Roy** – Spatiotemporal modeling of nuisance flood risk: a critical review of urban-scale approaches.
- **Emily Myers** – Relating air pollution, social vulnerability and health outcomes in 500 US cities.
- **Sarah Scheller** – Impacts of gentrification on garden land security.

## Current undergraduate students

- **Anna Koisewroski** – Honors thesis on redlining and the legacy of environmental degradation in Philadelphia and Pittsburgh.
- **Sophia Lemmons** – Philadelphia Garden Data Collaborative.

## Lab alumni · graduate

- **Luis Berrios-Hyden** – Spatial distribution of AMF diversity and heavy metal soil concentrations as a function of urban structure in Philadelphia.
- **Lucas Cummings** – Air pollution and urban structure in Philadelphia.
- **Kenny Douyon** – Land security assessment of community gardens in Philadelphia.
- **George Kowalski** – Supermarket use and sociodemographic distribution.
- **Kate Homet** – Measuring the impacts of environmental, social, and maintenance variables on green stormwater infrastructure in Philadelphia.
- **Radley Reist** – Comparative analysis of urban structure and surface temperature.
- **Charlotte Shade** – The use of green infrastructure and its effect on climate resilience in Philadelphia.

## Lab alumni · undergraduate

- **Madeline Scolio** – Senior thesis, now lead author on multiple peer-reviewed papers including Scolio et al. 2024 (*Urban Climate*) and Scolio et al. 2025 (*Sustainable Cities and Society*; *Environmental Research: Infrastructure and Sustainability*).
- **Alec Henderson** – Honors thesis on species distribution modeling of American chestnuts in Pennsylvania (Henderson, Santoro, Kremer 2022, *Frontiers in Ecology and Evolution*; Henderson et al. 2023, *Forest Ecology and Management*).
- **Kate Henderson** – Honors thesis on niche modeling of tody birds in the Greater Antilles (Henderson et al. 2022, *Frontiers in Ecology and Evolution*).
- **Rylie Fryberger** – Community garden closures in Philadelphia and the Philadelphia Community Gardens Survey.
- **Anna Johnson** – Food swamps and obesity in Indianapolis.
- **Gabi Manganello** – Flooding impacts of sea level rise on Long Beach Island, NJ.
- **Mary Corrigan** – Green space and obesity prevalence in San Diego and Phoenix.
- **Michael Donodeo** – Tourist demand for SEPTA stations during the 2026 World Cup.
- **Cameron Lomerson** – Remote sensing of air pollution in the Philadelphia metro before and during the COVID-19 pandemic.
- **Megan Gaughan** – Air pollution along a major urban artery.
- **Olivia Pistella** – Park quality and environmental justice.
- **Charlotte Bohra** – Air quality in Philadelphia from an environmental justice lens.
- **Kaitlyn Smith** – Crime rates and temperature in Philadelphia.
- **Marie Mullen** – Wind farm site suitability in Pennsylvania.
- **Brent Jenkins** – Surface temperature and air temperature in a peri-urban environment.
- **Halina Takahashi** – Urban structure and ecosystem services.
- **Beatriz De Jesus** – Sustainability perception assessment.
- **Kirsten Ahn** – Local flooding, heat, and health outcomes in Philadelphia.
- **Rebecca McDonough** – Air pollution analysis in Philadelphia.
- **Amira Temple** – Spatial distribution of the local food system in Philadelphia.
- **Meghan Conway** – Social vulnerability and air pollution.
- **Erik Mitz** – Urban structure and surface temperature in Philadelphia (Mitz et al. 2021, *Frontiers in Environmental Science*).
- **Ashley Seyfried** – Urban form and greenspace distribution: a nationwide spatial-temporal study.
- **Joe Dangler** – Morphological impacts of post-tropical cyclone Sandy on Long Beach, NY.
- **Meghan McMahon** – Fine-scale analysis of Philadelphia air pollution.
- **Alexandra Henninger** – Food production in urban farms.
- **Rachel Walsh** – Spatial patterns in organic farming and farm tenure across the USA.
- **Gavin Lewis** – Verification and analysis of community gardens in Philadelphia.
